race.name: Pragelato-Sestriere
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers between two Olympic venues — climbing from the Nordic stadium to the alpine summit on the tracks where champions raced in 2006.

vitals.aid_stations: 3 feed stations with warm drinks, energy food, and Italian treats

climate.description: Early March at high altitude in the Piedmont Alps. The course climbs from 1520m to over 2000m, ensuring reliable snow cover well into spring. Temperatures typically -8 to 0 degrees Celsius at the start, colder at the summit finish. The exposed high-altitude sections can produce strong winds.
challenges: Significant altitude gain from start to finish
Exposed high-altitude sections above 2000m with wind
Spring sun can soften south-facing sections
Altitude effects on breathing at the summit finish

course.primary: A 30km point-to-point course from the Pragelato Nordic stadium (2006 Olympic cross-country venue) climbing to the alpine resort of Sestriere. The route features sustained climbing through Olympic-grade terrain with a dramatic high-altitude finish. The course uses sections of the original 2006 Turin Winter Olympics cross-country tracks.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks on Olympic-standard trails with alpine terrain
features: Start at the 2006 Turin Winter Olympics Nordic venue
Finish at the iconic Sestriere alpine resort
High-altitude skiing above 2000m at the finish
Views of the Cottian Alps and Monte Viso

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong grooming score from Olympic-legacy infrastructure. Good scenery in the Cottian Alps. Limited by small field and the event's relatively young history. Olympic heritage adds unique character.

history.summary: Founded in 2008, two years after the 2006 Turin Winter Olympics brought world-class Nordic infrastructure to Pragelato. The race was created to make use of the Olympic cross-country venue and connect it to the famous alpine resort of Sestriere, which hosted Olympic alpine events. The point-to-point format from the Nordic stadium to the alpine summit creates a unique Olympic-legacy experience.
notable_facts: Pragelato hosted all cross-country skiing events at the 2006 Turin Winter Olympics
Sestriere hosted Olympic alpine events including giant slalom and slalom
The Olympic Nordic stadium at Pragelato is at 1,520m altitude
The course uses sections of the original Olympic cross-country trails
One of the few races that connects an Olympic Nordic venue to an Olympic alpine venue
